{
  "analysis": "The training data reveals a consistent pattern for German legal texts: Person entities appear in two main contexts. 1) As the subject of a complaint ('Beschwerdesache [Name]') or 'Beschwerde der/des [Name]'. 2) As the judge/official ('durch den Richter/die Richterin [Title] [Name]'). The names often include academic titles (Mag., Dr., Prof., etc.) and suffixes (LL.M., BA, BSc, etc.). The key challenge is distinguishing the person's name from surrounding text like addresses or titles. The pattern generally follows: [Title(s)] [First Name] [Last Name] [Optional Suffix]. The context 'in der Beschwerdesache' or 'durch den Richter/die Richterin' is crucial for disambiguation. Some entries have the name immediately following a preposition like 'des' or 'der'.",
  "strategy": "I will create two primary regex rules. Rule 1 targets names appearing after 'in der Beschwerdesache' or 'der/des' (genitive case) followed by a name pattern. Rule 2 targets names appearing after 'durch den Richter/die Richterin' (or variations like 'Ri', 'R.', etc.) followed by a name pattern. Both rules will use a capture group to extract the name including titles and suffixes, ensuring we capture the full entity text. The name pattern will be flexible to handle multiple titles, first names, last names, and suffixes.",
  "rules": [
    {
      "name": "person_complainant",
      "description": "Extracts person names appearing in the context of a complaint (Beschwerdesache, der/des Beschwerde).",
      "format": "regex",
      "pattern": "(?:in der Beschwerdesache|der|des)\\s+([A-Z][a-z]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:\\s+LL\\.M\\.|\\s+BA|\\s+BSc|\\s+B\\.A\\.|\\s+M\\.B\\.L\\.|\\s+MBA|\\s+Bakk\\. iur\\.)*|Mag\\.\\s+[A-Z][a-z]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:\\s+LL\\.M\\.|\\s+BA|\\s+BSc|\\s+B\\.A\\.)*|Dr\\.\\s+[A-Z][a-z]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:\\s+LL\\.M\\.|\\s+BA|\\s+BSc|\\s+B\\.A\\.)*|Hon\\.-Prof\\.(?:in)?\\s+[A-Z][a-z]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:\\s+LL\\.M\\.|\\s+BA|\\s+BSc|\\s+B\\.A\\.)*|Priv\\.-Doz\\.(?:in)?\\s+[A-Z][a-z]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:\\s+LL\\.M\\.|\\s+BA|\\s+BSc|\\s+B\\.A\\.)*|Univ\\.-Prof\\.(?:in)?\\s+[A-Z][a-z]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:\\s+LL\\.M\\.|\\s+BA|\\s+BSc|\\s+B\\.A\\.)*|Dipl\\.-Ing\\.(?:in)?\\s+[A-Z][a-z]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:\\s+LL\\.M\\.|\\s+BA|\\s+BSc|\\s+B\\.A\\.)*|ÖkR\\s+[A-Z][a-z]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:\\s+LL\\.M\\.|\\s+BA|\\s+BSc|\\s+B\\.A\\.)*|KzlR(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?)(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*)",
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$1",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities",
      "priority": 5,
      "reasoning": "Captures names following 'Beschwerdesache' or genitive cases like 'der/des'. Handles various titles and suffixes found in the training data."
    },
    {
      "name": "person_judge",
      "description": "Extracts person names appearing in the context of a judge/official ('durch den Richter/die Richterin').",
      "format": "regex",
      "pattern": "(?:durch den Richter|Richter|Ri|Ri1|Ri2|Ri3|Ri4|Ri5|Ri6|Ri7|Ri8|Ri9|Ri10|Ri11|Ri12|Ri13|Ri14|Ri15|Ri16|Ri17|Ri18|Ri19|Ri20|Ri21|Ri22|Ri23|Ri24|Ri25|Ri26|Ri27|Ri28|Ri29|Ri30|Ri31|Ri32|Ri33|Ri34|Ri35|Ri36|Ri37|Ri38|Ri39|Ri40|Ri41|Ri42|Ri43|Ri44|Ri45|Ri46|Ri47|Ri48|Ri49|Ri50|durch die Richterin|RichterinR|RichterinRi|durch die RichterinIBV)\\s+([A-Z][a-z]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?.*?)",
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$1",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities",
      "priority": 6,
      "reasoning": "Captures names following judge/official titles. The pattern is simplified to capture the name sequence after the title until a non-name character (like comma or space followed by address). This handles variations like 'Richter', 'Richterin', 'R.', etc."
    }
  ],
  "deleted_rules": []
}

{
  "analysis": "The training data reveals a consistent pattern for German legal documents: Person entities are almost exclusively preceded by specific titles (e.g., 'Mag.', 'Dr.', 'Prof.', 'Richter', 'Frau', 'Herr') or appear in specific syntactic positions (e.g., 'in der Beschwerdesache [Name]'). The entities often include suffixes like degrees ('Bakk. art.', 'MSc') or professional designations ('ÖkR', 'StR'). The main failure mode in generic NER is missing these titles or including the address that follows the name. The rules must prioritize the title+name structure and stop before the address or comma that typically follows.",
  "strategy": "I will create a set of regex rules that target specific title prefixes common in German legal texts. Each rule will use a capture group to isolate the name (including any trailing degrees) while ensuring the match stops before common delimiters like commas, addresses, or prepositions like 'von'. I will use a high priority for specific title patterns (Mag., Dr., Prof.) and a slightly lower priority for generic honorifics (Frau, Herr) to ensure precision.",
  "rules": [
    {
      "name": "academic_and_professional_titles",
      "description": "Matches names preceded by academic titles (Dr., Prof., Mag., Univ.-Prof., Priv.-Doz., Hon.-Prof.) and professional designations (ÖkR, StR, RgR, KommR, MedR, VetR). Handles gendered suffixes like 'in' or 'a'.",
      "format": "regex",
      "pattern": "(?:Dr\\.|Dr\\.in|Mag\\.|Mag\\.a|Univ\\.-Prof\\.|Univ\\.-Prof\\.in|Priv\\.-Doz\\.|Priv\\.-Doz\\.in|Hon\\.-Prof\\.|Hon\\.-Prof\\.in|ÖkR|StR|RgR|KommR|MedR|VetR|PhD|MSc|Bakk\\. art\\. MA|Bakk\\. rer\\. nat\\. MBA)\\s+([A-Z][a-zäöüß]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-zäöüß]+)+)",
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$1",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities",
      "priority": 10,
      "reasoning": "This is the most common pattern in the training data. It captures the full name including degrees but excludes the title itself. The pattern handles German umlauts and specific degree abbreviations found in the examples."
    },
    {
      "name": "generic_honorifics",
      "description": "Matches names preceded by generic honorifics 'Frau' or 'Herr' which are common in legal descriptions of parties.",
      "format": "regex",
      "pattern": "(?:Frau|Herr)\\s+([A-Z][a-zäöüß]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-zäöüß]+)+)",
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$1",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities",
      "priority": 8,
      "reasoning": "Covers cases like 'Frau Jennifer K\u00fcfner' or 'Herr \u00d6kR Samir Foellmer' where the title is a generic honorific rather than a specific academic/professional one."
    },
    {
      "name": "role_based_identification",
      "description": "Matches names following role indicators like 'Beschwerdef\u00fchrer', 'Beschwerdef\u00fchrerin', 'Beschuldigter', 'Schriftf\u00fchrer', 'Liquidator', 'Masseverwalter'.",
      "format": "regex",
      "pattern": "(?:Beschwerdef\u00fchrer|Beschwerdef\u00fchrerin|Beschuldigter|Schriftf\u00fchrer|Liquidator|Masseverwalter)(?:in)?(?:,|=|\\s+)([A-Z][a-zäöüß]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-zäöüß]+)+)",
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$1",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities",
      "priority": 7,
      "reasoning": "In legal texts, parties are often introduced by their role. This rule captures the name immediately following the role definition."
    },
    {
      "name": "post_position_names",
      "description": "Matches names appearing after prepositions like 'von', 'des', 'der' in contexts like 'von der Firma X' or 'des X Y' where X Y is a person.",
      "format": "regex",
      "pattern": "(?:von|des|der)\\s+([A-Z][a-zäöüß]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-zäöüß]+)+)(?:,|\\s|$)",
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$1",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities",
      "priority": 5,
      "reasoning": "Covers cases where the name appears after a preposition, often in phrases like 'von der Firma' (if the firm is named after a person) or 'des X Y'. This is less specific but catches edge cases."
    }
  ],
  "deleted_rules": []
}

metadata.analysis: The system suffers from catastrophic over-extraction due to overly permissive regex patterns that capture legal context words (e.g., 'Beschwerdesache', 'Finanzamtes') and partial titles as person names, resulting in a 76% false positive rate. The rules lack necessary negative lookahead assertions to prevent matching non-person nouns and fail to handle complex multi-title sequences (e.g., 'Mag. Mag. Hon.-Prof.') correctly.

metadata.guidance: Prioritize reducing the high false positive rate (2253 FP) by refining exclusion rules for background objects or textures that resemble humans. Introduce stricter spatial context constraints or multi-

messages.content:
{
  "analysis": "The primary failure mode is the 'legal_context_standalone_names' rule (priority 9) matching non-person nouns (e.g., 'Finanzamtes', 'Antrag', 'Bescheides', 'Entscheidungspflicht') because the pattern [A-Z][a-z]+ is too broad and matches any capitalized word following legal prepositions. Additionally, the rule fails to capture names in specific contexts like 'Die [Name] (Beschwerdeführerin)' or standalone names at the start of a sentence without a preceding title. The 'academic_and_professional_titles' rule works well for titles but misses names without titles. The 'generic_honorifics' rule is too restrictive with 'Herrn' and doesn't handle 'Herr' + Name correctly in all cases. The 'names_after_role_prepositions' rule has similar over-matching issues.",
  "strategy": "1. Replace 'legal_context_standalone_names' with a more restrictive rule that requires a comma or specific sentence structure after the name to avoid matching common nouns like 'Finanzamtes'. 2. Add a specific rule for names appearing after 'Die' (feminine) or 'Der' (masculine) followed by a role in parentheses (e.g., 'Die Heike Osteroth (Beschwerdeführerin)'). 3. Add a rule for names appearing at the start of a sentence or after a colon/period without a title, but only if followed by a comma or specific legal context. 4. Tighten the 'generic_honorifics' rule to ensure it captures the full name correctly. 5. Add a rule for names following 'in der Beschwerdesache' or 'in der Rechtssache' that are not preceded by a title, ensuring the match stops before the address (comma).",
  "rules": [
    {
      "name": "legal_context_standalone_names",
      "description": "Captures names appearing after specific legal context phrases like 'in der Beschwerdesache', 'gegen', 'des', 'der' when no title is present. Requires a comma or end of phrase to avoid matching common nouns.",
      "format": "regex",
      "content": "(?:in der Beschwerdesache|in der Rechtssache|in der Revisionssache|des Beschwerdeführers|der Beschwerdeführerin|des Beschuldigten|der Beschuldigten|gegen|des|der|durch den Richter|durch die Richterin)\\s+(?!(?:Dr\\.|Dr\\.in|Mag\\.|Mag\\.a|Univ\\.-Prof\\.|Univ\\.-Prof\\.in|Priv\\.-Doz\\.|Priv\\.-Doz\\.in|Hon\\.-Prof\\.|Hon\\.-Prof\\.in|ÖkR|StR|RgR|KommR|MedR|VetR|PhD|MSc|Bakk\\. art\\. MA|Bakk\\. rer\\. nat\\. MBA|LLB|LLM|BEd|Ing\\.|RA|OMedR|Senatsvorsitzende|HR|OSR)\\s+)([A-Z][a-zäöüß]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-zäöüß]+)*)(?=,|\\s+[A-Z][a-zäöüß]+\\s+[0-9]|\\s+\\n|\\s*$)",
      "priority": 9,
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$1",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities"
    },
    {
      "name": "names_after_role_prepositions",
      "description": "Captures names following role indicators like 'Beschwerdeführer', 'Beschwerdeführerin', 'Beschuldigter', 'Schriftführer', 'Liquidator', 'Masseverwalter', or role abbreviations like 'OSR'. Captures the full name including suffixes. Requires comma or end of phrase.",
      "format": "regex",
      "content": "(?:des Beschwerdeführers|der Beschwerdeführerin|des Beschuldigten|der Beschuldigten|des Schriftführers|des Liquidators|des Masseverwalters|OSR|StR|RgR|KommR|MedR|VetR)\\s+(?!(?:Dr\\.|Dr\\.in|Mag\\.|Mag\\.a|Univ\\.-Prof\\.|Univ\\.-Prof\\.in|Priv\\.-Doz\\.|Priv\\.-Doz\\.in|Hon\\.-Prof\\.|Hon\\.-Prof\\.in|ÖkR|StR|RgR|KommR|MedR|VetR|PhD|MSc|Bakk\\. art\\. MA|Bakk\\. rer\\. nat\\. MBA|LLB|LLM|BEd|Ing\\.|RA|OMedR|Senatsvorsitzende|HR)\\s+)([A-Z][a-zäöüß]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-zäöüß]+)*)(?=,|\\s+[A-Z][a-zäöüß]+\\s+[0-9]|\\s+\\n|\\s*$)",
      "priority": 8,
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$1",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities"
    },
    {
      "name": "names_after_die_der_role",
      "description": "Captures names appearing after 'Die' or 'Der' followed by a role in parentheses (e.g., 'Die Heike Osteroth (Beschwerdeführerin)').",
      "format": "regex",
      "content": "(?:^|\\s)(?:Die|Der)\\s+([A-Z][a-zäöüß]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-zäöüß]+)*)(?=\\s*\\()",
      "priority": 8,
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$1",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities"
    },

The pattern needs to be adjusted to capture the title sequence as part of the entity text, or a separate rule must be added to prepend titles to the name match. Currently, it produces partial entities.",
    "3c97e99c": "This rule has high False Positives (171) because it matches 'Beschwerdesache [Name]' and 'Stadt [Name]'. The negative lookahead for titles is present, but the context words 'von', 'des', 'der' are too common and often precede non-person nouns in legal texts. Add a specific negative lookahead for 'Beschwerdesache' and 'Rechtssache' immediately before the name. Also, ensure the pattern does not match if the preceding word is a location prefix.",
    "a4cbf0aa": "This rule matches 'Begründung Hilde Heinsohn' as a person. The pattern `^...` is too loose. It needs to strictly anchor to the start of a sentence or a specific legal header, and exclude common document section headers like 'Begründung', 'Zum Vorbringen', 'Entscheidung'. Add a negative lookahead for these specific headers.",
    "13773682": "This rule has 0 matches. The pattern is likely too restrictive or the negative lookahead for titles is preventing valid matches. Review the logic: it tries to match names after roles but excludes titles. If the text contains 'des Beschwerdeführers Dr. Name', the negative lookahead `(?!(?:Dr\\. ...))` will fail to match 'Name' because 'Dr.' is present. This rule logic seems inverted or the exclusion list is too broad for the intended use case.",
    "4f2eb727": "This rule is identical to `13773682` (except for the suffix handling) and also has 0 matches. It is redundant and ineffective. Consider merging or removing it.",
    "34ef8480": "This rule has 0 matches. The pattern `(?:Herr|Frau)\\s+[A-Z][a-zäöüß]+\\s+([A-Z]...)` expects a first name before the last name. In German legal texts, 'Herr' is often followed by the title and last name (e.g., 'Herr Dr. Müller') or just the last name. The pattern is too specific to 'Herr [FirstName] [LastName]'. Adjust to allow for titles or just the last name: `(?:Herr|Frau)\\s+(?:[A-Z][a-zäöüß]+\\s+)?([A-Z][a-zäöüß]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-zäöüß]+)?)`.",
    "3c97e99c": "See above regarding False Positives."
  },
  "task_guidance": "1. **Entity Span Definition**: The system is failing to define the 'Person' entity as the *full* name including titles (e.g., 'Dr. Name', 'Mag. Name'). You must prioritize rules that capture the full span (Title + Name) over rules that capture just the name. 2. **Contextual Filtering**: The most critical missing logic is a robust negative lookahead for legal context words that are NOT part of the name, specifically 'Beschwerdesache', 'Rechtssache', 'Revisionssache', and location prefixes ('Stadt', 'Gemeinde'). These must be excluded from the capture group or the match entirely. 3. **Priority Reordering**: Currently, broad rules like `standalone_name_in_list` (Priority 7) and `legal_context_standalone_names` (Priority 9) are causing massive FPs. You should lower the priority of these broad 'list' rules and raise the priority of 'Title + Name' rules. 4. **Rule Consolidation**: You have multiple rules doing similar things (e.g., `names_after_role_prepositions` variants). Consolidate them into a single, well-tested pattern that handles titles, roles, and context correctly. 5. **Post-Processing**: Consider adding a post-processing step to filter out matches that contain known non-person keywords (like 'Beschwerdesache') or are clearly locations, as regex alone is struggling with the ambiguity of German legal syntax."
}

metadata.analysis: The system suffers from catastrophic over-matching due to rules that capture entire phrases (e.g., 'Beschwerdesache Name') instead of just the name, and a failure to capture titles/suffixes which leads to low recall and fragmented entities. The priority system is ineffective because the 'broad' rules (like `standalone_name_in_list`) run with high priority and capture non-person text, while the 'precise' rules fail to match the full entity span.

messages.content:
You are the Refinement Coordinator for a rule learning system.
After each refinement iteration, you analyze per-class performance and guide the next patch.

ITERATION: 1/1
OVERALL: accuracy=3.6%, micro_F1=12.1%, macro_F1=12.1%

PER-CLASS PERFORMANCE (sorted worst to best):
  person: F1=12% P=13% R=11% (TP=138 FP=926 FN=1083)

Return JSON:
{
  "focus_classes": ["list of class names that need the most improvement"],
  "guidance": "Specific advice for the rule generator — which classes to prioritize, what patterns to try, what to avoid. 2-3 sentences max.",
  "should_continue": boolean (false if performance is good enough or unlikely to improve further)
}

{
  "analysis": "The primary failure mode is the inclusion of the context word 'Beschwerdesache' (and similar legal terms) into the entity span, and the misclassification of location names (e.g., 'Wiener Neustadt', 'Stadt Wien') as persons. The 'standalone_name_in_list' rule is too broad and triggers on almost any capitalized sequence following a comma or space. The 'legal_context_standalone_names' rule incorrectly captures the context prefix. The 'embedded_titles_and_suffixes' rule fails to capture the title itself, only the name. The 'names_after_role_prepositions' rule has inverted logic (negative lookahead preventing valid matches).",
  "strategy": "1. **Delete** the overly broad 'standalone_name_in_list' rule to eliminate the source of 900+ FPs. 2. **Delete** 'legal_context_standalone_names' as it is fundamentally flawed in its capture group logic. 3. **Delete** 'names_after_role_prepositions' and 'names_after_role_prepositions_no_suffix' due to inverted logic and redundancy. 4. **Delete** 'names_at_text_start' and 'names_after_herr_frau' as they are too specific or prone to FPs in this context. 5. **Replace** 'embedded_titles_and_suffixes' with a new rule that correctly captures the full 'Title + Name' span using a positive lookahead for the name structure, ensuring the title is part of the match. 6. **Add** a new rule 'names_after_legal_context' that strictly matches names following legal context phrases (like 'in der Beschwerdesache') but uses a negative lookbehind to ensure the context word is NOT included in the capture, and explicitly excludes location prefixes. 7. **Add** a new rule 'names_after_role_markers' to handle cases like 'durch den Richter [Title] [Name]' where the title is present, ensuring the full span is captured.",
  "rules": [
    {
      "name": "names_after_legal_context",
      "description": "Captures person names appearing after legal context phrases like 'in der Beschwerdesache', 'in der Rechtssache', etc. Uses negative lookbehind to exclude the context word and negative lookahead to exclude location prefixes.",
      "format": "regex",
      "pattern": "(?<!\\w)(?:in der Beschwerdesache|in der Rechtssache|in der Revisionssache|des Beschwerdeführers|der Beschwerdeführerin|des Beschuldigten|der Beschuldigten|gegen|des|der|durch den Richter|durch die Richterin)\\s+(?!(?:Stadt|Gemeinde|Land|Bezirk|Finanzamt|Bundesfinanzgericht)\\s+[A-Z])\\s+([A-Z][a-zäöüß]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-zäöüß]+)*(?:\\s+(?:Bakk\\. rer\\. nat\\.|Bakk\\. art\\. MA|LLM|LLB|Ing\\.|VetR|OMedR|RA|StR|RgR|KommR|MedR|ÖkR)\\s+)?[A-Z][a-zäöüß]+)?(?=,|\\s+[A-Z][a-zäöüß]+\\s+[0-9]|\\s+\\n|\\s*$)",
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$1",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities",
      "priority": 10,
      "reasoning": "Replaces the broken 'legal_context_standalone_names'. Uses negative lookbehind (?<!\\w) to ensure we don't capture the context word itself. Explicitly excludes location prefixes like 'Stadt', 'Gemeinde' to prevent FPs like 'Stadt Wien'. Captures the full name including suffixes."
    },
    {
      "name": "embedded_titles_and_suffixes",
      "description": "Matches names preceded by academic/professional titles (Dr., Mag., RgR, etc.) and captures the FULL span including the title. Handles multiple titles.",
      "format": "regex",
      "pattern": "((?:Dr\\.|Dr\\.in|Mag\\.|Mag\\.a|Univ\\.-Prof\\.|Univ\\.-Prof\\.in|Priv\\.-Doz\\.|Priv\\.-Doz\\.in|Hon\\.-Prof\\.|Hon\\.-Prof\\.in|\\u00d6kR|StR|RgR|KommR|MedR|VetR|PhD|MSc|Bakk\\. art\\. MA|Bakk\\. rer\\. nat\\. MBA|LLB|LLM|BEd|Ing\\.|RA|OMedR|HR|OSR|MMag\\.Dr\\.)\\s+(?:[A-Z][a-zäöüß]+\\s+)*[A-Z][a-zäöüß]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-zäöüß]+)*(?:\\s+(?:Bakk\\. rer\\. nat\\.|Bakk\\. art\\. MA|LLM|LLB|Ing\\.|VetR|OMedR|RA|StR|RgR|KommR|MedR|\\u00d6kR)\\s+)?[A-Z][a-zäöüß]+)?(?=,|\\s+[A-Z][a-zäöüß]+\\s+[0-9]|\\s+\\n|\\s*$)",
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$1",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities",
      "priority": 12,
      "reasoning": "Fixes the previous rule that only captured the name. Now captures the title + name. The pattern allows for multiple titles and suffixes. The lookahead ensures we stop before addresses or legal terms."
    },
    {
      "name": "names_after_role_markers",
      "description": "Captures names following generic prepositions (von, des, der, durch) and optional honorifics (Herrn, Frau), or specific role markers. Handles cases where the name is immediately after the preposition.",
      "format": "regex",
      "pattern": "(?:von|des|der|durch)\\s+(?:Herrn?|Frau(?:n)?|Dr\\.|Mag\\.|Univ\\.-Prof\\.|Univ\\.-Prof\\.in|Priv\\.-Doz\\.|Hon\\.-Prof\\.|\\u00d6kR|StR|RgR|KommR|MedR|VetR|PhD|MSc|Bakk\\. art\\. MA|Bakk\\. rer\\. nat\\. MBA|LLB|LLM|BEd|Ing\\.|RA|OMedR|Senatsvorsitzende|HR|OSR|MMag\\.Dr\\.)?\\s+([A-Z][a-zäöüß]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-zäöüß]+)+)(?=,|\\s+[A-Z][a-zäöüß]+\\s+[0-9]|\\s+\\n|\\s*$|\\s+\\w+\\s+\\d+)",
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$1",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities",
      "priority": 8,
      "reasoning": "Refines the previous rule to handle 'Herr/Frau' followed by titles or just names. The lookahead ensures we don't match locations or other non-person entities."
    },
    {
      "name": "names_at_text_start",
      "description": "Captures names appearing at the very beginning of the text or after a newline, often in case headers or signatures, excluding common document section headers.",
      "format": "regex",
      "pattern": "^(?:IM NAMEN DER REPUBLIK\\s+)?(?!(?:Begründung|Zum Vorbringen|Entscheidung|Sachverhalt|I\\.|II\\.|III\\.))([A-Z][a-zäöüß]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-zäöüß]+)+)(?=\\s+in\\s+der|\\s+der\\s+Beschwerdesache|\\s+des\\s+Beschwerdeführers|\\s+vertreten|\\s+als|\\s+\\n|\\s*$)",
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$1",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities",
      "priority": 12,
      "reasoning": "Adds negative lookahead to exclude document headers like 'Begründung' which were causing FPs. Anchors to start of text or newline."
    }
  ],
  "deleted_rules": [
    "standalone_name_in_list",
    "legal_context_standalone_names",
    "names_after_role_prepositions",
    "names_after_role_prepositions_no_suffix",
    "names_after_herr_frau"
  ]
}
